value.market_read: Headline: A massive 8.3 mb crude draw pulled total US stocks to 418.2 mb as refiners ran hard, lifting utilization 1.4 pts to 96.7% and crude inputs to 17.19 mb/d. Cushing fell another 1.6 mb to 20.0 mb — already below the ~25 mb operational floor and deteriorating fast, down 5.8 mb over five weeks from 25.82 mb on May 15. Driver: The crude draw was front-loaded in PADD II (-4.5 mb) and PADD III (-4.8 mb) as Gulf Coast and Midwest plants ran near peak. The 4-week crude export pace eased to 4.87 mb/d from 5.57 mb/d while imports slipped to 5.66 mb/d, so the draw is a domestic-run story, not an export-pull story — barrels are being consumed by the refining system, not shipped out. Trade flows: Products were mixed: gasoline drew 0.9 mb to 214.2 mb against a firm 8.95 mb/d 4-week demand (up from 8.86), jet drew 1.1 mb, while distillate built 0.95 mb to 103.1 mb as soft 3.73 mb/d demand failed to absorb supply from elevated runs. Watch next week: Cushing deliverability is the dominant risk — at 20.0 mb and falling, prompt WTI backwardation should steepen and tank-bottoms stress intensifies. Watch whether the crude draw moderates as runs approach a ceiling at 96.7% utilization and whether distillate keeps building into soft demand.

padd.padd1: Distillate led the East Coast build, up 0.42 mb as runs recovered. PADD I utilization jumped 3.1 pts to 87.1%, the strongest regional util gain outside the West Coast, lifting product output across the slate. Gasoline was essentially flat (+0.04 mb) and jet drew 0.99 mb, the steepest regional jet draw on the week. With national gasoline demand firm at 8.95 mb/d, NYH RBOB cash structure governs whether Colonial pulls barrels north — recent backwardation near 19 c/gal would accelerate any PADD I build. Watch: NYH RBOB spot-vs-forward — sustained backwardation lifts Colonial nominations and rebuilds gasoline; a flip to contango stalls supply and tightens the East Coast.
padd.padd2: Cushing fell another 1.61 mb to 20.03 mb — already well below the ~25 mb operational floor and down 5.8 mb in five weeks, a deliverability red flag. The broader PADD II crude draw of 4.51 mb reflects refiners running flat-out, with utilization up 2.9 pts to a stretched 101.2%. That run intensity built distillate by 1.02 mb and gasoline by 0.27 mb. With tanks this far sub-floor, prompt WTI backwardation should steepen as the market prices physical tightness at the hub. Watch: Cushing tank bottoms — at 20 mb any further draw deepens deliverability stress; monitor WTI prompt spreads for blowout backwardation signaling barrels are getting hard to source.
padd.padd3: Gulf Coast crude drew 4.83 mb as refiners held near peak at 96.2% utilization, even with util ticking down 1.2 pts. Gasoline drew 1.97 mb — the largest regional product move — while distillate eased 0.34 mb and jet built 0.21 mb. The 4-week export pace slowed to 4.87 mb/d from 5.57 mb/d, so the crude draw is run-driven rather than export-driven, leaving less slack to absorb a supply shock. Watch: Hurricane season (June 1 start) against near-peak runs — any Gulf storm threat would whipsaw both crude draws and product cracks; track LOOP sour differentials and Asian buying for export-pace direction.
padd.padd4: Rockies crude drew 0.35 mb with utilization firm at 98.4%, up 0.9 pts near the top of its range. Product moves were minor — gasoline +0.10 mb, distillate +0.08 mb, jet flat — consistent with steady regional runs and no turnaround disruption. Bakken and DJ basin barrels continue to feed local plants without signs of stress. Watch: Any unplanned outage at the small PADD IV plant base — utilization this high leaves little cushion, and a single unit trip can swing tight regional gasoline balances fast.
padd.padd5: West Coast utilization surged 8.1 pts to 93.4%, the sharpest regional gain, driving a 1.35 mb crude build as plants restarted runs post-turnaround. The higher run rate lifted gasoline by 0.65 mb, while distillate drew 0.23 mb and jet eased 0.12 mb. CARB-spec isolation keeps LA Basin margins sensitive to this run swing. Watch: Whether the util surge holds and rebuilds CARB gasoline cover into summer driving demand; monitor Rodeo conversion effects on regional diesel/jet balances and LA Basin spot margins.
